screen.notes: Profile; career data; billing info. Billing routing: /billing/* from settings UI.

claim_rows.claim: PUT /api/agents/providers/anthropic/credential (and the per-user PUT /api/agents/user/providers/anthropic/credential) auto-detect and accept two credential formats: a Console API key (sk-ant-api03-...) sent via x-api-key header, OR a Claude Code OAuth token (sk-ant-oat01-...) sent via Authorization: Bearer + anthropic-beta: oauth-2025-04-20 header
claim_rows.method: Submit both credential formats via the agent-config credential UI/API on prod; inspect stored authMode and confirm the correct auth header is used on the subsequent live call

claim_rows.claim: The in-app interactive "Connect with Anthropic" OAuth-consent flow remains removed and unsupported (only paste-token credential entry exists)
claim_rows.method: Inspect agent-monitor/agents/settings UI for absence of an interactive OAuth-consent button; curl GET/POST /api/agents/auth/anthropic/start expecting 404

claim_rows.claim: AETHER_ALLOWED_INTERNAL_EMAIL_DOMAINS (default aether.local) is an exact-domain allowlist: an aether.local settings email is accepted; a different domain sharing only the .local label (e.g. evil.local / sub.aether.local) is rejected with 422
claim_rows.method: Attempt to save a settings email at aether.local (expect success/200) and at evil.local or sub.aether.local (expect 422)

claim_rows.claim: PUT /api/workspaces/settings persists an email change (a prior defect where the UPDATE "User" statement silently omitted the email field, returning a false-positive HTTP 200, has been fixed) -- confirmed via direct production-DB read
claim_rows.method: Change the settings email via UI/API, then GET the settings back (and/or a direct DB read per DEPLOYMENT-RUNBOOK.md) to confirm the new value persisted

claim_rows.claim: All 27 core Phase-7 gates were VERIFIED-CLOSED including: 0 console errors across 20 routes (GATE-16), 0 same-origin 5xx (GATE-17), backend 676 pytest passed (GATE-19), frontend 297 vitest passed (GATE-20), Playwright E2E green (GATE-21)
claim_rows.method: Playwright console/network sweep across the 20 documented routes on prod; re-run backend pytest / frontend vitest suites (suite-run for counts); manual click-through spot-check for E2E flows

claim_rows.claim: A reserved-TLD email outside the allowlist (e.g. sub.aether.local) now correctly returns 422; an allowlisted domain (aether.local) returns 200 and persists (GATE-09)
claim_rows.method: Attempt saving a non-allowlisted reserved-TLD email, confirm 422; attempt an allowlisted domain, confirm 200+persist

claim_rows.claim: All users are backfilled to the Free plan with a quota row (GATE-34)
claim_rows.method: Check /billing/entitlement for an existing/fresh user account, confirm Free-plan default and a quota row exists

claim_rows.claim: A Playwright sweep of 14 dashboard routes + /pricing + /admin as a paid admin showed 0 console errors / 0 failed requests / 0 page errors (GATE-03)
claim_rows.method: Playwright sweep of the listed routes on prod, capture console + network errors

claim_rows.claim: Stripe billing round-trip (checkout, webhook, GST invoice, ABN/Tax) is BLOCKED-ON-HUMAN pending operator-supplied Stripe test keys, webhook secret, Price IDs, and ABN/Tax enrollment (GATE-13/14/15/16/33)
claim_rows.method: Attempt POST /billing/checkout on prod; observe whether it succeeds (would indicate Stripe now configured, contradicting this claim) or fails/degrades honestly (would confirm still-blocked)

claim_rows.claim: Subscription pricing: 4 tiers -- Free A$0 / Starter A$19 / Pro A$39 / Power A$69 monthly; A$179/359/649 annual; GST-inclusive AUD with gst = round(total/11, 2)
claim_rows.method: GET /billing/plans on prod (or view /pricing), confirm tier names/prices match; compute GST via the stated formula on a sample total and compare to the displayed value

baseline_note: Baseline captures from Step 6 are under uat/reports/evidence/manual-verification/screens/ (folder named settings or settings_auth/settings_unauth or baseline/settings depending on sweep layout) — use as the 'before' record.
